PROTOCOL AMENDMENT #3
Study NCT00000001: A Phase III Study of Experimental Drug X in Adults with Type 2 Diabetes
Effective Date: March 15, 2024

1. RATIONALE FOR AMENDMENT

This amendment broadens the eligibility criteria based on interim safety data and
recommendations from the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) review conducted on
February 20, 2024. The DSMB concluded that the safety profile supports enrollment
of a wider patient population.

2. SUMMARY OF CHANGES

2.1 Inclusion Criteria Changes:
  - Upper age limit increased from 65 to 75 years to include elderly patients
    who showed favorable response in the Phase II extension study.
  - HbA1c upper limit raised from 10.0% to 11.0% to capture patients with
    moderately uncontrolled diabetes who may benefit most.
  - BMI range widened from 25-40 to 22-45 kg/m2 to include a broader
    metabolic phenotype spectrum.

2.2 Exclusion Criteria Changes:
  - Renal threshold relaxed from eGFR < 60 to eGFR < 45 mL/min/1.73m2,
    reflecting accumulated safety data in patients with mild-to-moderate
    chronic kidney disease.

3. EXPECTED IMPACT ON ENROLLMENT

Based on registry analysis, these changes are projected to increase the eligible
patient pool by approximately 35%, potentially adding 120 additional participants
across all sites. The expanded age range alone accounts for an estimated 15% increase.
